goal: Generate a slow archival map-sequence study that orients the viewer to Jerusalem, Judea, and the broader Rome-facing geography for recurring documentary use.

visual_requirements.tone: archival, cinematic, tactile, scholarly, geographically legible
visual_requirements.color_notes: aged parchment base, muted gold route lines, restrained deep red markers, subdued green only as a minor accent
visual_requirements.composition_notes: slow camera travel, one destination emphasis per shot, clean labels, generous negative space, readable first and last frames
must_include: regional geography around Judea or Palestine
one restrained route or locator system
usable start and end frames for editing

text_inputs.secondary_text: Judea, Temple context, and Rome-facing regional orientation

review_checks: Jerusalem remains visually dominant and readable
Motion feels deliberate rather than trailer-like
Could be reused in more than one episode
No obvious historical or stylistic contradiction
destination_after_approval: visual-library/01-core-collections/maps/curated/ for approved references, or visual-library/02-series-kits/motion-presets/ if the motion study becomes a reusable system reference.
run_notes: Best paired with the video-generation-template.md prompt. Save the selected prompt and output notes in 05-ai-automation/runs/ before promoting anything.
